title: Smoky Roasted Kenyan Chicken Curry
description: A light yet smoky Kenyan-inspired chicken curry with roasted chicken, creamy single cream sauce, and a hint of chili for a lazy Sunday dinner.
instructions: Pat the chicken thighs dry with paper towels and season generously with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven‑proof skillet over medium‑high heat. Sear the chicken thighs skin‑side down until golden, about 3–4 minutes, then flip and sear the other side for 2 minutes.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast the chicken for 25–30 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 75 °C (165 °F).

While the chicken roasts, combine the diced onion, minced garlic, tomato paste, diced tomatoes, and chicken stock in a saucepan. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat and let the mixture thicken for 10 minutes.
Add the single cream to the pan, stir until smooth, and season with salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon juice if desired.
Remove the chicken from the oven and slice into strips. Return the sliced chicken to the sauce, stirring to coat fully.
Place the skillet back in the oven, switch to broil, and broil for 2–3 minutes to caramelize the tops lightly.
Serve the smoky chicken curry over steamed rice or gluten‑free flatbread, and garnish with freshly chopped cilantro.
Enjoy a comforting, smoky, and creamy Kenyan chicken curry perfect for a lazy Sunday evening.
